metadata.title: Guideline on clinical investigation of medicinal products for the treatment of sepsis

rules.rule_text: The scope of this guideline is restricted to drug therapy preventing organ failure and death as complications of the underlying infectious disease. It does not cover antibiotics, treatment of underlying conditions, or supportive care.
rules.verbatim_quote: The scope of the present document is restricted to drug therapy preventing organ failure and death, which ultimately develop in sepsis as complications of the underlying infectious disease, and will not consider other drugs intended to be used in the treatment of underlying condition and supportive care.

rules.rule_text: Patient inclusion must use validated scoring systems: descriptive scores (SOFA, MODS, GCS, LOD, ODIN) and/or prognostic scores (APACHE, SAPS, MPM) to define severity. APACHE or SAPS scores are advised for comparisons across studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Validated scores should be used prospectively for definition of the inclusion criteria. In order to allow comparisons across studies it is advised to use APACHE or SAPS score apart from the scale preferred, both of which are validated for providing predicted mortality data in the targeted population.

rules.rule_text: An infectious cause must be confirmed by direct or indirect means, differentiating from non-infectious causes of SIRS. Missing identification of the etiological agent is not an exclusion criterion, but the highest level of circumstantial evidence of infection should be sought.
rules.verbatim_quote: Differentiation from non-infectious causes of SIRS by direct or indirect confirmation of an infectious cause of the disease including anatomical site of its focus... Missing of the etiological agent is not an exclusion criterion. The highest level of circumstantial evidence of infection should be sought in cases where direct proof of an infectious cause is not available.

rules.rule_text: Patients must be categorised at entry according to age, source of infection (post-surgery, medical, community-acquired), type of organism, severity score, and time-point of treatment intervention, for subsequent stratification or subgroup analysis.
rules.verbatim_quote: the broad patient population at entry should be categorised according to various parameters including age, source of infection (e.g. post-surgery, medical, community acquired), type of infection/organism, underlying disease, co-morbidity, pre-medication, severity score and time-point of treatment intervention, for subsequent analysis of prospectively defined subgroups or a priori stratification.

rules.rule_text: Concomitant therapy (antibiotics, fluids, inotropes, vasopressors, organ support, surgical treatment) must be comparable among treatment groups, with careful recording and documentation. Standard therapy must be standardised as far as possible across study sites.
rules.verbatim_quote: These measures should be comparable among treatment groups to be investigated, with careful recording and documentation, and should be standardised as far as possible... standard therapy in main therapeutic studies needs to be clearly defined... It is recommended that the results are stratified according to the participating centres.

rules.rule_text: Short-term (28-day) all-cause mortality should be the primary efficacy endpoint in clinical trials of severe sepsis/septic shock. This must be supported by 3-months mortality data and preferably by long-term mortality data at a minimum of 6 months.
rules.verbatim_quote: All cause mortality is the most relevant endpoint in clinical trials of severe sepsis/septic shock. Short-term (28-day) all cause mortality should be the primary efficacy endpoint in studies assessing the efficacy of drugs in patients with life-threatening acute illnesses... the 28-days primary mortality endpoint should be supported by 3-months mortality data and preferably also by long-term mortality data at a minimum of 6 months.
rules.exceptions: For treatments aimed at preventing organ failure in low-mortality sepsis populations, other parameters than mortality might substitute, subject to regulatory advice.

rules.rule_text: Secondary efficacy endpoints should include in-hospital mortality, ICU length of stay, hospital length of stay, and other parameters of sepsis morbidity. Key dynamic markers of activity (inflammatory markers, coagulation/fibrinolysis activation) should be included.
rules.verbatim_quote: In-hospital mortality, ICU length of stay, and hospital length of stay are secondary efficacy endpoints as well as other parameters of sepsis morbidity... To corroborate findings in exploratory studies, it is advisable to include key dynamic markers of activity.

rules.rule_text: Quality of life should be assessed using validated scores (e.g. SF-36) for at least 3 and up to 12 months. Data on neurological function at 6 months after randomisation should also be provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: In long-term trials quality of life could be assessed by use of validated scores, e.g. the SF-36 questionnaire. In this case, evaluation should be performed for at least 3 and up to 12 months. It is also recommended to provide data with regard to neurological function 6 months after randomisation.

rules.rule_text: Drug-drug interaction studies with drugs likely to be used in sepsis (antibiotics, inotropes, vasopressors, etc.) must be appropriately investigated.
rules.verbatim_quote: interactions with various drugs likely to be used in sepsis should be appropriately investigated following the existing guidance (Note for Guidance on the Investigation of Drug Interactions, CPMP/EWP/560/95).

rules.rule_text: In critically ill patients, PK is often altered by impaired organ function and extracorporeal circulation. Drug/metabolite levels or indirect pharmacodynamic parameters should be obtained and used in dose-response studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: In critical care patients, pharmacokinetics are often altered not only due to changes to drug metabolism and excretion by impaired organ function, but also due to extracorporeal circulation of blood with in many cases unpredictable effects on drug elimination. Therefore, drug/metabolite levels or indirect indication of some parameter of the drug's pharmacodynamics should be obtained.

rules.rule_text: Efficacy of a new sepsis treatment must be established in randomised, double-blind, controlled studies. Efficacy should generally be demonstrated in comparison with placebo (both as add-on to standard therapy), since no active comparator with a firmly established treatment effect size exists.
rules.verbatim_quote: Efficacy of a new treatment in sepsis should be established in randomised, double-blind, controlled studies... In general, efficacy should be demonstrated in comparison with placebo. Currently, no active comparator is available for which the treatment effect size has been firmly established. Therefore a non-inferiority margin cannot be defined
rules.exceptions: Where an active comparator with proven effective superiority exists, superiority must also be shown.

rules.rule_text: Dose and duration of drug administration should reflect flexible rather than fixed dosing, determined by patient parameters based on pharmacologically plausible results from phase II dose-finding studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: The relevance of pharmacokinetic considerations in critically ill patients should be reflected in flexible rather than fixed dosing determined by patient parameters based on pharmacologically plausible results from phase II dose-finding studies.

rules.rule_text: Pre-defined analysis of subgroups by prognostically relevant factors (demographics, disease severity, concomitant treatment, biochemical markers) is recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: Pre-defined analysis of subgroups is recommended according to prognostically relevant factors as well as other parameters that play a role in the clinical management of the disease including demographics, disease severity, concomitant treatment and biochemical markers.

rules.rule_text: A new drug for paediatric sepsis should be investigated in separate clinical studies in paediatric age groups as defined in ICH E11 (including neonates). Companies are encouraged to seek regulatory advice for the paediatric development plan.
rules.verbatim_quote: a new drug intended for the treatment of sepsis in the paediatric population should be investigated in separate clinical studies in those age groups as defined in ICH E11... especially also in the neonates. Due to the complexity of all aspects to be considered a detailed recommendation for the design of these trials would be beyond the scope of the present guideline.

rules.rule_text: Safety of all patients should generally be surveyed by a Data Monitoring Committee. Special emphasis should be given to AEs predicted by the pharmacodynamic properties of the investigational product. Groups at increased risk of AEs must be identified and appropriate safety measures for vulnerable populations warranted.
rules.source_section: 5.1 Specific adverse events to be monitored
rules.verbatim_quote: Safety of all patients should generally be surveyed by a Data Monitoring Committee... Special emphasis should be put on AE predicted by the pharmacodynamic properties of the investigational product... Any groups at increased risk of AE should be identified. Appropriate ways of observing safety for trials in such vulnerable patient populations are warranted.
